expected_failure_modes: Agent hovers high without descending because r_progress becomes zero when vy=0, but r_centering and r_stability are too weak to force descent; solved by enough weight on r_progress.
Agent crashes fast because r_progress encourages high descent speed; clipped by tanh saturation and by r_soft_touchdown penalty on hard impact.
Agent touches down gently but not centered because r_centering penalty is zero at high altitude; the near‑ground scaling ensures centering becomes strict as landing approaches.
Agent uses side engine to spin instead of translating, because r_stability only penalises angle; solved by action cost on side_engine and centering pressure that makes spinning counter‑productive.
risk_notes: r_centering scaling (1 - clip(y,0,1)) can be negative if y<0; assuming the environment keeps y≥0, this is safe.

r_soft_touchdown fires on single‑leg ground contact, which may train very conservative landing approaches (hovering on one leg); but the large terminal bonus for dual‑contact with strict criteria discourages that.
tanh(-vy) in r_progress provides limited gradient for large negative vy; the saturating shape may slow early learning of fast descent, but guarantees bounded reward.
